name: TechEmpower — Multiple Queries (20 per request)
notes: TFB multiple-queries test at 20 individual single-row reads per request (each fetched separately — 'not acceptable to execute multiple SELECTs within a single complex query'), 512 concurrency. IDENTICAL to the single-query model but for a ratio(20) transform on the svc->db wire, so the DB sees 20x the request rate and saturates at ceiling/20. This is the 20x structural law SDA must reproduce. The DB ceiling here is the FIT ANCHOR (5900 x 20 = 118,000 q/s); db.postgres.perRequestDuration is SHARED with the single-query entry. The framework's CPU station (cpuCores/cpuTimePerRequestMs) is declared too (shared with single-query) but is INERT here: at 20 queries per request the DB saturates at 5,900 req/s, far below the framework's CPU ceiling (~104,000), so this entry stays DB-bound and pins the DB service time cleanly while single-query pins the framework CPU.

sources.note: TFB test-requirements wiki: multiple-queries = 20 individual single-row reads per request, run at 512 concurrency; the reported column is 20
Round 22 hardware (Citrine): the 28-thread Xeon Gold 5120 DB box shared by every framework

tunables.note: SAME tunable id as the single-query entry (db.postgres:perRequestDuration) — one shared free variable. This 20-query point is the clean ANCHOR for it: the DB saturates at 5,900 req/s = 118,000 q/s / 20, fixing the service time at ~0.2373 ms. Single-query no longer fights it for the fit, because the framework CPU (a SEPARATE resource) now binds the single-query ceiling instead of the DB.
PINNED to the 28 hardware threads (as in the single-query entry) so the DB fit degree of freedom is exactly one shared service time.
The framework's CPU width, PINNED to 28 (Citrine app box hardware threads). Shared with the single-query entry; inert here (the DB binds first at 20 queries/request).

The framework's per-request CPU time (shared free variable with the single-query entry). Constrained only by single-query — here the DB ceiling (5,900) sits far below the framework CPU (~104,000), so this entry does not bind on CPU and the shared value is free to serve single-query without disturbing the 20-query DB fit.

groundTruth.note: spring-mongo 20-query peak (Round 22). 5900 x 20 = 118,000 q/s at the DB — the same DB work as the 104k single-query run. This is the FIT ANCHOR for the DB rate; SDA's job is to reproduce the /20 structural relationship (5900 = ceiling/20), which it does exactly.
